file_name: OTISWORLDWIDECORP_04_03_2020-EX-10.4-INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AGREEMENT by and among UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION, OTIS WORLDWIDE CORPORATION and CARRIER ~1.txt

perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Information" shall have the meaning defined in Section 6.2.
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Information" shall have the meaning defined in Section 6.2 and shall only include data explicitly marked as confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause requiring data to be explicitly marked as confidential to be considered Confidential Information. This contradicts the original definition in Section 6.2, which includes information 'identified as or are of the type generally considered as confidential or proprietary' without requiring explicit marking. Thus, data considered confidential by its nature but not explicitly marked may now be excluded, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Confidential Information. All Received Information and Materials that are identified as or are of the type generally considered as confidential or proprietary or that have historically been subject to reasonable confidentiality and proprietary protections, and any communications or information provided after the Effective Time pursuant to this Agreement among members of the different Party Groups, will be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the Person that provided it, unless the information (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure in breach of this Agreement; (b) is rightfully available to or known by the receiving Party prior to receipt by the receiving Party 12 without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) is received by the receiving Party from a third party, provided that the third party is not known by the receiving Party, after reasonable inquiry, to be in breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) was independently developed by the receiving Party, without violating any contractual or legal obligation ("Confidential Information").
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a requirement that Confidential Information must be explicitly marked as confidential, contradicting Section 6.2, which defines Confidential Information to include data that is 'identified as or are of the type generally considered as confidential or proprietary' without requiring explicit marking. This creates a direct conflict because data considered confidential by its nature under 6.2 may now be excluded if not explicitly marked, making the contract internally inconsistent and creating an ambiguity. Therefore, according to the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction,' a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 9.4 Amendments and Waivers. This Agreement may not be modified or amended, except by an instrument or instruments in writing signed by the Party against whom enforcement of any such modification or amendment is sought.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.4 Amendments and Waivers. This Agreement may not be modified or amended, except by an instrument or instruments in writing signed by all Parties. However, waivers of any provision can be granted unilaterally by any Party, effective immediately upon notification to other Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction regarding amendments versus waivers. The original text requires written agreements signed by the Party against whom enforcement is sought for ALL modifications. The added text allows unilateral waivers with immediate effect. This creates a contradiction because a Party might argue that a unilateral 'waiver' effectively 'amends' the agreement in practice, and questions whether or not those unilateral waivers are really valid given the first sentance. This affects the enforceability of any provision subject to a purported unilateral waiver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.4 Amendments and Waivers. This Agreement may not be modified or amended, except by an instrument or instruments in writing signed by all Parties. However, waivers of any provision can be granted unilaterally by any Party, effective immediately upon notification to other Parties. The waiver by any Party to this Agreement of a breach of any term or provision of this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of any subsequent breach. No failure or delay by any Party in exercising any right or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege. Subject to Section 3.3, the rights and remedies herein provided shall be cumulative and not exclusive of any rights or remedies provided by law.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires amendments to be signed by the party against whom enforcement is sought, while the changed text allows unilateral waivers effective immediately. This creates a direct contradiction because a waiver can effectively amend the agreement in practice, raising questions about its validity given the initial requirement for signed amendments. This meets the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it creates interpretational challenges regarding the enforceability of provisions subject to unilateral waivers, directly conflicting with the process for amendments.
